title: Polestar First Quarter Loss Widens Tariffs Pricing Pressure
text: Polestar Q1 2026 results show losses widening on tariffs and pricing pressure despite continued volume growth. Company delivered 13,126 vehicles, 7% increase from prior year, yet net loss ballooned to $383 million from $166 million year-over-year, more than doubling. Gross margin swung to negative 3.2% from positive 10.3% in Q1 2025, 13.5 percentage point deterioration. Revenue remained nearly flat at $633 million versus $632 million, showing flat growth despite increased volumes. Volume-to-revenue disconnect highlights aggressive pricing necessary to drive sales. Adjusted EBITDA worsened to loss of $235 million versus loss of $96 million year-over-year. Drivers of margin compression include intensified pricing pressure in European market, newly implemented EU and U.S. tariffs, declining carbon credit revenue from $29 million to $21 million and shifting product mix toward lower-margin Polestar 4 vehicles. Cash position declined from $1,159 million at year-end 2025 to $676 million at quarter-end. Management attributed challenges to very competitive market environment.
